title: Do not trust distributed locks for correctness
description: Treat Redis locks as efficiency hints, not correctness guarantees; guard critical operations with fencing or idempotency.
body: Use when reaching for a Redis lock to serialize a critical operation. Do not assume a SET NX lock (or Redlock) guarantees mutual exclusion: GC pauses, clock skew, and failover can let two holders believe they own it, and a TTL that fires mid-operation releases the lock to another worker. Actions: use the lock only to reduce contention, and make the protected operation safe even if two run — via a fencing token checked at the source of truth or an idempotent write. Always set SET key val NX PX <ttl> and delete only if you still own it (compare value via Lua). Validation signals include a test where a lock expiring mid-operation does not corrupt the protected resource because the write is idempotent/fenced. Risk boundary: a Redis lock as the sole guard for money/inventory is unsafe; escalate and require fencing.
